title: Implement hunk-state-aware diff parsing for correct symbol classification
context: Adversarial review revealed diff analyzer misclassified content lines starting with ++/-- as file headers, causing incorrect symbol state tracking (false 'changed' labels, missed pure renames).
decision: Redesigned diff analyzer to track parse state (header vs. hunk content sections), classify symbols per-file rather than globally, and detect pure renames. Added 6 regression tests covering header-collision, renames, and cross-file moves.
consequences: Symbol moves between files now tracked as add+remove per-file, not incorrectly as 'changed' within a file
Pure renames (100% similarity, no content change) now detected and recorded separately
Regression suite prevents re-introduction of these misclassifications
Store and context operations also hardened (jsonStore.delete on raw JSON, context assembly depth bounds)
alternatives_rejected: Single-pass line-by-line regex without state tracking (insufficient for unambiguous classification)
Accept false positives in symbol classification and defer fix
